message:
feat(core,dir,btrfs,ondisk): expand btrfs chunk tree, subvol root dirid, and dir proptest coverage

The btrfs mount path now walks the chunk tree to expand beyond the
bootstrap sys_chunk_array, giving correct logical→physical mappings for
multi-device and large filesystems. The subvolume root directory inode
is resolved from the root tree instead of being hardcoded.

- ffs-core: walk_chunk_tree expansion with fallback to sys_chunk_array
  if the chunk tree is unreadable; resolve subvol_root_dirid from root
  tree item for correct root inode lookup
- ffs-dir: add proptest-based property tests for add_entry alignment
  across all name lengths, directory block geometry, and round-trip
  invariants; add helper strategies and assertion macros
- ffs-btrfs: expose walk_chunk_tree and related helpers for chunk map
  expansion from chunk tree leaves
- ffs-ondisk: extend btrfs on-disk type coverage for chunk tree parsing
- ffs-cli: adapt to new BtrfsContext subvol_root_dirid field
- ffs-fuse: adapt to updated BtrfsContext initialization
- ffs-harness: add btrfs_kernel_reference test suite; extend capability
  drift detection for chunk tree and subvol root behaviors
- Update CHANGELOG.md, FEATURE_PARITY.md, and beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -3,8 +3,8 @@
 All notable changes to FrankenFS are documented in this file, organized by capability area rather than chronological diff order. This project has no formal releases or tags; development has been continuous since inception. Commit links point to the canonical GitHub repository.
 
 > **Repository:** <https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenfs>
-> **Period covered:** 2026-02-09 through 2026-03-21
-> **Total commits:** 536
+> **Period covered:** 2026-02-09 through 2026-04-06
+> **Total commits:** 670
 
 ---
 
@@ -81,6 +81,18 @@ Pure, I/O-free parsing of ext4 on-disk structures in `ffs-ondisk`. All parsers t

+- **Feature-compatibility and metadata-layout corrections** -- casefold lookup, relaxed incompat acceptance, external-journal UUID pairing, kernel-compatible bitmap CRC32C handling, and ext4 xattr field layout fixes all tightened ext4 compatibility

+- **Broader checked-conversion and panic-removal sweep** -- more silent truncations, panicking integer casts, and ad hoc literals were replaced with fallible conversions and explicit error paths across `core`, `extent`, `alloc`, `types`, and `cli`
